use_cases: Building readable log or print messages with variable values
Formatting numbers with decimal places or padding
examples:
items = 5
print(f'You have {items} item{"s" if items != 1 else ""}')

use_cases: Passing a simple function to sorted(), map(), or filter()
Defining a throwaway function inline without def
Creating key functions for sorting complex objects
Wrapping file I/O or network calls that might fail
Validating user input without stopping the program
Catching specific errors and providing fallback behavior

stash.description: Temporarily save uncommitted changes without committing
use_cases: Switching branches without committing half-done work
Setting aside experimental changes to test something else

rebase.description: Reapply commits on top of another base branch
use_cases: Keeping a feature branch up to date with main
Squashing multiple commits into one before merging
Creating a linear, cleaner commit history
